title: Reading notes
text:
Protocol-adjacent papers worth annotating in depth. Not every paper — just the ones that directly inform how you run an experiment or interpret a result.

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
text:
Configure experiment


Code
viewof n_samples  = Inputs.number({label: "Number of samples", value: 4, min: 1, max: 24, step: 1})
viewof n_cells_M  = Inputs.number({label: "Cells per sample (×10⁶)", value: 5, min: 0.75, step: 0.25})
viewof pcr_cycles = Inputs.number({label: "PCR cycles (target 7–8)", value: 8, min: 4, max: 12, step: 1})
viewof cell_type  = Inputs.select(["Adherent", "Suspension", "Semi-adherent"], {label: "Cell type"})
































Code
md`**${n_samples} sample(s) · ${n_cells_M}M cells · ${cell_type} · ${pcr_cycles} PCR cycles**`

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
text:
Scaled master mixes






NoteMath audit — how every volume is calculated





Core formula (all standard mixes):
\[V_{total} = V_{per\_sample} \times N_{samples} \times 1.10\]
Formaldehyde crosslinking — working backwards from 1% final in ~10 mL/sample:
\[V_{16\%\;stock} = \frac{N_{cells} \times 1.1}{0.16} \; mL\]
Glycine quench — to reach 0.2M final from 2M stock:
\[V_{2M\;glycine} = \frac{N_{cells} \times 1.1}{10} \; mL\]
Biotin fill-in — ⚠️ 5% excess ONLY (not 10%):
\[V_{total} = V_{per\_sample} \times N_{samples} \times \mathbf{1.05}\]
Biotin-14-dATP costs ~$800/vial. The 5% exception is intentional and hardcoded.





Code
fmt = (x, d = 1) => x.toFixed(d)
e   = n_samples * 1.10
e5  = n_samples * 1.05























Lysis buffer master mix (1 mL/sample)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "1M Tris-HCl pH 8.0", "Per sample (µL)": 10,  "Total (µL)": fmt(10  * e)},
  {Component: "5M NaCl",             "Per sample (µL)": 2,   "Total (µL)": fmt(2   * e)},
  {Component: "IGEPAL CA-630",       "Per sample (µL)": 2,   "Total (µL)": fmt(2   * e)},
  {Component: "H₂O",                 "Per sample (µL)": 986, "Total (µL)": fmt(986 * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})







Lysis buffer + protease inhibitor (300 µL/sample)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "Lysis buffer",       "Per sample (µL)": 250, "Total (µL)": fmt(250 * e)},
  {Component: "Protease inhibitor", "Per sample (µL)": 50,  "Total (µL)": fmt(50  * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})













Warning



Biotin fill-in — 5% excess ONLY. Biotin-14-dATP is very expensive. Do not apply the standard 10% excess.


Biotin fill-in master mix (50 µL/sample — 5% excess only)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "0.4 mM Biotin-14-dATP",        "Per sample (µL)": 37.5, "Total — 5% excess ONLY (µL)": fmt(37.5 * e5)},
  {Component: "10 mM dCTP",                    "Per sample (µL)": 1.5,  "Total — 5% excess ONLY (µL)": fmt(1.5  * e5)},
  {Component: "10 mM dGTP",                    "Per sample (µL)": 1.5,  "Total — 5% excess ONLY (µL)": fmt(1.5  * e5)},
  {Component: "10 mM dTTP",                    "Per sample (µL)": 1.5,  "Total — 5% excess ONLY (µL)": fmt(1.5  * e5)},
  {Component: "Klenow (5 U/µL, NEB M0210L)",  "Per sample (µL)": 8,    "Total — 5% excess ONLY (µL)": fmt(8    * e5)}
], {layout: "auto"})







Ligation master mix (900 µL/sample)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "ddH₂O",                                   "Per sample (µL)": 673, "Total (µL)": fmt(673 * e)},
  {Component: "10× NEB T4 ligase buffer",                 "Per sample (µL)": 120, "Total (µL)": fmt(120 * e)},
  {Component: "10% Triton X-100",                         "Per sample (µL)": 100, "Total (µL)": fmt(100 * e)},
  {Component: "BSA (20 mg/mL)",                           "Per sample (µL)": 6,   "Total (µL)": fmt(6   * e)},
  {Component: "T4 DNA Ligase (2000 U/µL, NEB M0202M)",   "Per sample (µL)": 1,   "Total (µL)": fmt(1   * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})







Crosslink reversal master mix (546 µL/sample)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "ddH₂O",                    "Per sample (µL)": 432, "Total (µL)": fmt(432 * e)},
  {Component: "Proteinase K (20 mg/mL)",  "Per sample (µL)": 18,  "Total (µL)": fmt(18  * e)},
  {Component: "10% SDS",                  "Per sample (µL)": 50,  "Total (µL)": fmt(50  * e)},
  {Component: "5M NaCl",                  "Per sample (µL)": 46,  "Total (µL)": fmt(46  * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})







End repair master mix (100 µL/sample — Day 5)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "1× NEB T4 ligase buffer",  "Per sample (µL)": 88, "Total (µL)": fmt(88 * e)},
  {Component: "25 mM dNTP mix",           "Per sample (µL)": 2,  "Total (µL)": fmt(2  * e)},
  {Component: "T4 PNK (10 U/µL)",         "Per sample (µL)": 5,  "Total (µL)": fmt(5  * e)},
  {Component: "T4 DNA Pol (3 U/µL)",      "Per sample (µL)": 4,  "Total (µL)": fmt(4  * e)},
  {Component: "Klenow (5 U/µL)",          "Per sample (µL)": 1,  "Total (µL)": fmt(1  * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})







dATP attachment master mix (100 µL/sample — Day 5)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "1× NEB Buffer 2",              "Per sample (µL)": 90, "Total (µL)": fmt(90 * e)},
  {Component: "10 mM dATP",                   "Per sample (µL)": 5,  "Total (µL)": fmt(5  * e)},
  {Component: "Klenow exo minus (5 U/µL)",    "Per sample (µL)": 5,  "Total (µL)": fmt(5  * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})









Code
md`**Streptavidin bead prep (${n_samples * 150} µL total — Day 5)**`









Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "Streptavidin T1 beads (10 mg/mL)", "Per sample (µL)": 150, "Total (µL)": fmt(150 * e)},
  {Component: "1× Tween wash buffer",              "Per sample (µL)": 400, "Total (µL)": fmt(400 * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})









Code
md`**PCR master mix (${pcr_cycles} cycles — Day 6)**`









Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "PCR primer cocktail", "Per sample (µL)": 5,  "Total (µL)": fmt(5  * e)},
  {Component: "Enhanced PCR mix",    "Per sample (µL)": 20, "Total (µL)": fmt(20 * e)}
], {layout: "auto"})

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
text:
Day 0 — Crosslinking






WarningFormaldehyde — fume hood only



All formaldehyde steps must be performed in a fume hood. Collect formaldehyde-exposed waste separately for disposal per institutional guidelines. Glycine quench must be added in the hood before moving tubes to bench.








TipCounting cells before crosslinking





Plate an extra well at the same density as your experimental wells and count from that well on Day 0. Do not count from your experimental wells — removing cells for counting changes the density of the remaining cells.





Code
fa = (n_cells_M * 1.1 / 0.16).toFixed(2)
gl = (n_cells_M * 1.1 / 10).toFixed(2)

Inputs.table([
  {Step: "1", Action: `Count cells; assume ~${n_cells_M}M/sample`,                                          Detail: cell_type},
  {Step: "2", Action: `Add 1% formaldehyde (${fa} mL of 16% stock/sample); 10 min RT`,                     Detail: "Fume hood; rock gently"},
  {Step: "3", Action: `Quench: ${gl} mL 2M glycine/sample → 0.2M final; 5 min RT`,                         Detail: "Rock; RT"},
  {Step: "4", Action: "Pellet; wash 5 mL cold PBS × 2; scrape if adherent",                                 Detail: "2500 rpm 5 min 4°C"},
  {Step: "5", Action: `Resuspend 1 mL PBS/5M cells; aliquot ${n_cells_M}M/tube`,                            Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "6", Action: "Pellet 900×g 5 min 4°C; remove supernatant",                                         Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "7", Action: "Flash freeze in LN₂; store –80°C",                                                    Detail: "—"}
], {layout: "auto"})





























ImportantChecklist




Formaldehyde waste in designated fume hood containers
Glycine quench added in fume hood
LN₂ flash freeze complete; pellets at –80°C

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
section: Day 1 — Lysis & MboI restriction digest (~2 hr + O/N)
text:
Day 1 — Lysis & MboI restriction digest (~2 hr + O/N)






WarningMboI handling



Keep MboI in the glycerol block on ice at all times. Return to –20°C immediately after dispensing. Never let it sit at room temperature — glycerol-containing enzymes lose activity quickly when warmed.








NoteWhy 0.5% SDS + Triton X-100 quench?





SDS permeabilizes nuclei to allow MboI access to chromatin. However, SDS also inhibits MboI, so it must be quenched with Triton X-100 before adding enzyme. The 62°C step ensures denaturation of surface proteins without denaturing chromatin structure.











Step
Action
Detail




1
Set microcentrifuge 4°C; thermomixer 62°C; oven 37°C
—


2
Thaw crosslinked cells on ice; add 300 µL lysis buffer + PI
Ice 15 min


3
Spin 2500×g 5 min 4°C; discard supernatant
—


4
Wash pellet with 500 µL lysis buffer; spin again
—


5
Resuspend in 50 µL 0.5% SDS; 62°C 7 min
—


6
Add 145 µL ddH₂O + 25 µL 10% Triton X-100; quench SDS
37°C 15 min; avoid foaming


7
Add 25 µL 10× NEBuffer 2 + 20 µL MboI (100 U)
Return MboI to –20°C immediately


8
Digest O/N (or ≥2 hr) at 37°C with rotation
Hybridization oven









ImportantChecklist




MboI returned to –20°C
Samples rotating at 37°C O/N

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
section: Day 2 — Biotin fill-in, ligation & crosslink reversal (~6.5 hr + O/N)
text:
Day 2 — Biotin fill-in, ligation & crosslink reversal (~6.5 hr + O/N)






Warning5% excess only for biotin fill-in — not 10%



Biotin-14-dATP is extremely expensive. The biotin fill-in master mix uses 5% excess (not the standard 10%). This is the only step in the protocol with a different excess factor.








NoteWhy proximity ligation at RT with slow rotation?





Proximity ligation must occur while chromatin is still condensed inside intact nuclei — this ensures only DNA ends that are spatially close get ligated. Elevated temperature or vigorous mixing would release chromatin and allow random intermolecular ligation, destroying the contact information.











Step
Action
Detail




1
Inactivate MboI: 62°C 20 min; cool to RT
Set oven to 23°C (open door)


2
Add 50 µL biotin fill-in mix — 5% excess only
37°C 1.5h rotation


3
Add 900 µL ligation mix; RT 4h slow rotation
Slowest oven speed; door open


4
Spin 2500×g 5 min; remove supernatant
—


5
Resuspend in 546 µL crosslink reversal mix; 55°C 30 min
Thermomixer


6
Continue 68°C O/N; no shaking
—









ImportantChecklist




Biotin fill-in time: _______ min at 37°C
Ligation time: _______ hr at RT
68°C O/N started at: _______

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
section: Day 3 — DNA precipitation (~3 hr + 6 hr at –80°C)
text:
Day 3 — DNA precipitation (~3 hr + 6 hr at –80°C)






TipThis is a clean break point



DNA can sit dissolved in 1× Tris pH 8 at 4°C O/N after the precipitation step. If you need to pause, stop here — Day 4 shearing can be done the next morning.










Step
Action
Detail




1
Cool tubes to RT
—


2
Add 874 µL 100% EtOH + 55 µL 3M NaAc pH 5.2
Invert to mix; –80°C 4–6h


3
Spin max speed 2°C 25 min; remove supernatant on ice
Keep tubes cold throughout


4
Wash 800 µL 70% EtOH × 2; vacuum dry
—


5
Dissolve in 130 µL 1× Tris pH 8; 37°C 1h
Or 4°C O/N









ImportantChecklist




–80°C precipitation started at: _______
Qubit BR dsDNA before shearing: _______ ng/µL

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
text:
Day 4 — Shearing & size selection (~6.5 hr)






WarningTarget size is 300–500 bp — verify with gel before proceeding



If shearing is outside this range, the size selection will be off and library complexity will suffer. Run a 1:5 dilution on a 2% agarose gel before proceeding to AMPure. Do not skip the gel check.








TipAMPure size selection logic





The two-step AMPure protocol selects for 300–500 bp fragments. Step 1 (0.55×): large fragments (>500 bp) bind beads, small fragments (<500 bp) stay in supernatant — keep supernatant. Step 2 (add 0.15× more to supernatant): now 300–500 bp fragments bind — keep these beads. Fragments <300 bp remain in supernatant and are discarded.











Step
Action
Detail




1
Qubit BR; if >150 ng/µL dilute to 150 ng/µL in 130 µL
—


2
Covaris LE220: Duty 25 / PIP 500 / 200 cyc/burst / 90 sec
130 µL microTUBE; fill level 6


3
Transfer + wash vial with 70 µL ddH₂O → 200 µL total
—


4
Gel verify shearing: 1:5 dilution on 2% agarose
Use bottom layer of gel


5
AMPure 0.55× (110 µL); keep supernatant
<500 bp in supernatant


6
Add 0.15× more AMPure (30 µL) to supernatant; keep beads
300–500 bp on beads


7
Wash 700 µL 70% EtOH × 2; elute in 300 µL 1× Tris
—


8
Qubit HS dsDNA; gel verify size selection
Store –20°C









ImportantChecklist




Post-shear gel: _______ pass/fail
Post-size-selection Qubit: _______ ng/µL
Size range confirmed: _______ bp

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
section: Day 5 — Biotin pull-down & library prep (~9 hr)
text:
Day 5 — Biotin pull-down & library prep (~9 hr)






WarningAdapter index tracking — record before opening tubes



Write down your sample-to-index assignment now, before you open any adapter tubes. Index swaps are catastrophic and invisible until sequencing.



Sample
Index

























Code
Inputs.table([
  {Step: "1", Action: `Prep strep T1 beads: ${n_samples * 150} µL total in ${Math.ceil(n_samples/4)} tube(s)`, Detail: "Wash 1× TWB; resuspend in 2× binding buffer"},
  {Step: "2", Action: "Add 300 µL washed beads/sample; 15 min RT rotation",    Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "3", Action: "Magnet; wash 600 µL 1× TWB 55°C 2 min × 2",            Detail: "450 rpm"},
  {Step: "4", Action: "End repair: 100 µL master mix; RT 30 min",              Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "5", Action: "Wash 1× TWB 55°C × 2; resuspend in 1× NEB Buffer 2",   Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "6", Action: "dATP attachment: 100 µL master mix; 37°C 30 min",       Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "7", Action: "Wash 1× TWB 55°C × 2; resuspend in Quick ligation buffer", Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "8", Action: "Add 3 µL UNIQUE adapter + 2 µL Quick ligase; RT 15 min", Detail: "⚠ Record sample–index below"},
  {Step: "9", Action: "Wash 1× TWB 55°C × 2; resuspend in 50 µL 1× Tris",    Detail: "Store beads + DNA at –20°C"}
], {layout: "auto"})













ImportantChecklist




All adapter assignments recorded above
Beads + DNA stored at –20°C

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
section: Day 6 — PCR amplification & final cleanup (~2 hr)
text:
Day 6 — PCR amplification & final cleanup (~2 hr)






TipCycle number





Target 7–8 cycles for 5M cells starting input. Use 4–6 cycles if starting input was higher (>10M cells). Never exceed 12 cycles — PCR duplicates inflate apparent contact counts and reduce unique pair complexity.





Code
Inputs.table([
  {Step: "1", Action: `25 µL DNA+beads + 25 µL PCR mix; ${pcr_cycles} cycles`, Detail: "95°C 3 min → (98°C 20s/60°C 15s/72°C 30s) × N → 72°C 5 min"},
  {Step: "2", Action: "Add 200 µL 1× Tris → 250 µL; magnet; transfer supernatant", Detail: "Save beads at –20°C"},
  {Step: "3", Action: "AMPure 0.7× (175 µL); wash 700 µL 70% EtOH; resuspend 100 µL", Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "4", Action: "Second AMPure 0.7× (70 µL); wash × 2; elute 25 µL 1× Tris", Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "5", Action: "Qubit HS dsDNA; Tapestation", Detail: "Submit for sequencing"}
], {layout: "auto"})













ImportantChecklist




Final Qubit: _______ ng/µL
Tapestation peak: _______ bp (expected 300–600 bp)
Submitted for sequencing: _______

text:

smear 200–2000 bp

title: In situ Hi-C Protocol
text:
References
Rao SSP et al. Cell. 2014;159(7):1665–1680. Protocol: Phanstiel lab; adapted by Katie Metz, HyunAh Kim, Yoseli Quiroga, JP Flores.

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
text: 🖨 Print / Save as PDF

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
text:
Configure experiment


Code
viewof n_samples  = Inputs.number({label: "Number of samples", value: 4, min: 1, max: 48, step: 1})
viewof n_cells_M  = Inputs.number({label: "Cells per sample (×10⁶)", value: 0.5, min: 0.1, step: 0.1})
viewof antibody   = Inputs.select(["H3K4me3 (positive ctrl)", "H3K27me3 (positive ctrl)", "IgG (negative ctrl)", "CTCF", "SOX10", "BRG1", "CHD7", "Custom"], {label: "Primary antibody target"})
viewof pcr_cycles = Inputs.number({label: "PCR cycles", value: 12, min: 10, max: 15, step: 1})
































Code
md`**${n_samples} sample(s) · ${n_cells_M}M cells each · antibody: ${antibody} · ${pcr_cycles} PCR cycles**`

TipBuffer storage





NE1 and Wash buffer can be made as a stock (without protease inhibitor) and stored at 4°C for up to 2 weeks. Add protease inhibitor only to the volume you need immediately before use. Stop buffer can be stored at 4°C for 1 month — add RNAse A fresh before use.

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
text:
Scaled master mixes






NoteMath audit — how every volume is calculated





Core formula (all standard master mixes):
\[V_{total} = V_{per\_sample} \times N_{samples} \times 1.10\]
SDS / Proteinase K mix (4 µL added per sample after Stop buffer):
\[V_{SDS} = 2 \; \mu L \times N \times 1.10 \qquad V_{PK} = 2 \; \mu L \times N \times 1.10\]
ConA bead prep (not scaled with excess — fixed 10 µL per sample):
\[V_{beads} = 10 \; \mu L \times N_{samples}\]
Where N = your current input for number of samples. Include controls as samples.





Code
e_cnr = n_samples * 1.10
fmt_cnr = (x, d = 1) => x.toFixed(d)
















SDS / Proteinase K (add 4 µL/sample after Stop buffer)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "10% SDS",                   "Per sample (µL)": 2, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(2 * e_cnr)},
  {Component: "Proteinase K (20 mg/mL)",   "Per sample (µL)": 2, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(2 * e_cnr)}
], {layout: "auto"})







End repair & A-tailing (add 10 µL to 50 µL sample)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "End Repair & A-tailing Buffer",      "Per sample (µL)": 7, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(7 * e_cnr)},
  {Component: "End Repair & A-tailing Enzyme mix",  "Per sample (µL)": 3, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(3 * e_cnr)}
], {layout: "auto"})







Adapter ligation (add 45 µL mix + 5 µL unique adapter)


Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "PCR-grade H₂O",   "Per sample (µL)": 5,  "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(5  * e_cnr)},
  {Component: "Ligation Buffer", "Per sample (µL)": 30, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(30 * e_cnr)},
  {Component: "DNA Ligase",      "Per sample (µL)": 10, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(10 * e_cnr)}
], {layout: "auto"})









Code
md`**PCR amplification (${pcr_cycles} cycles)**`









Code
Inputs.table([
  {Component: "KAPA HiFi HotStart ReadyMix (2×)",          "Per sample (µL)": 25, "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(25 * e_cnr)},
  {Component: "KAPA Library Amplification Primer Mix (10×)", "Per sample (µL)": 5,  "Total (µL)": fmt_cnr(5  * e_cnr)}
], {layout: "auto"})

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
section: Day 1 — Cell binding to ConA beads (~2 hr)
text:
Day 1 — Cell binding to ConA beads (~2 hr)






NoteWhy ConA beads?





Concanavalin A-coated beads bind to the cell surface via glycoproteins, immobilizing cells for all subsequent washes. This eliminates centrifugation steps that would pellet and stress the nuclei. The key is that cells remain membrane-intact throughout — you are not isolating nuclei.





Code
Inputs.table([
  {Step: "1", Action: "Thaw pellets on ice; resuspend in NE1 buffer",                             Volume: "1000 µL/sample"},
  {Step: "2", Action: "Rotate 10 min 4°C",                                                         Volume: "—"},
  {Step: "3", Action: "Spin 500×g 10 min 4°C; resuspend in Wash buffer",                          Volume: "1000 µL/sample"},
  {Step: "4", Action: `Activate ConA beads: ${(10 * n_samples).toFixed(0)} µL total`,             Volume: "Wash 1× Binding Buffer"},
  {Step: "5", Action: `Resuspend in ${(10 * n_samples).toFixed(0)} µL Binding Buffer`,            Volume: "10 µL beads/sample"},
  {Step: "6", Action: "Mix cells + beads; invert ~10×; 10 min RT; magnet",                        Volume: "Resuspend in 50 µL Antibody Buffer/sample"},
  {Step: "7", Action: "Add primary antibody; O/N 4°C thermomixer 1000 rpm",                       Volume: "Per Ab dilution sheet"}
], {layout: "auto"})













ImportantChecklist




Primary antibody: _______ — lot # _______
IgG negative control included
H3K4me3 positive control included
Samples in thermomixer at 4°C, 1000 rpm O/N

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
text:
Day 2 — pAG-MNase & chromatin digestion (~4 hr)






WarningCaCl₂ addition triggers digestion immediately



Once CaCl₂ is added, MNase is active. Work quickly and consistently — keep all samples on ice until the moment you add CaCl₂ to each tube, then incubate exactly 30 min at 4°C. Longer digestion = smaller fragments = loss of signal at broad domains.










Step
Action
Detail




1
Pulse spin; magnet; wash Triton Buffer
1 mL/sample


2
Repeat Triton Buffer wash
1 mL/sample


3
Resuspend in Antibody Buffer; add pAG-MNase
50 µL + 2.5 µL/sample


4
Incubate 1h 4°C metal block
—


5
Wash × 2 Triton Buffer; resuspend in Triton Buffer
100 µL/sample


6
Add 100 mM CaCl₂; 30 min 4°C to digest
2 µL/sample


7
Add 2× Stop Buffer; 20 min 37°C to release fragments
100 µL/sample


8
Magnet; transfer supernatant to new tube
Fragments are in supernatant


9
Add SDS/Prot-K master mix; 1h 65°C (or O/N)
4 µL/sample


10
Zymo Clean & Concentrator; elute in 55 µL
Lo-Bind tube; –20°C









ImportantChecklist




CaCl₂ added; digestion timer started
37°C incubation complete
Supernatant transferred cleanly (beads discarded)
DNA in Lo-Bind tube at –20°C









TipLow yield after Zymo cleanup?





Ensure you are collecting the supernatant after CaCl₂ digestion — the released fragments are in solution, not on the beads. Also check that the Zymo column was not overloaded (max 5 µg DNA per column).

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
text:
Day 3 — Library preparation (~4 hr)






WarningAdapter index tracking



Record your adapter–sample assignment before you open any tubes. Adapter swaps are not detectable until sequencing data arrives. Use a unique index for each sample — never reuse an index within a run.




Code
Inputs.table([
  {Step: "1", Action: "Thaw KAPA reagents at 4°C",                                                    Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "2", Action: "End repair & A-tailing: add 10 µL mix to 50 µL sample",                        Detail: "20°C 30 min → 65°C 30 min (heated lid 85°C)"},
  {Step: "3", Action: "Adapter ligation: add 45 µL mix + 5 µL UNIQUE adapter",                        Detail: "20°C 15 min; check adapter compatibility sheet"},
  {Step: "4", Action: "AMPure round 1 (1.2×): 132 µL beads → elute 50 µL 10 mM Tris",               Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "5", Action: "AMPure round 2 (1.2×): 60 µL beads → elute 25 µL",                            Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "6", Action: `PCR: 30 µL master mix + 20 µL sample; ${pcr_cycles} cycles`,                  Detail: "98°C 45s → (98°C 15s/60°C 10s) × N → 72°C 1 min"},
  {Step: "7", Action: "Post-PCR AMPure (1.2×) → elute 25 µL",                                         Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "8", Action: "Qubit 1× HS dsDNA; Tapestation D1000",                                          Detail: "Store 4°C (2 wk) or –20°C"}
], {layout: "auto"})













TipHow many PCR cycles?





10–12 cycles for abundant targets (H3K4me3, H3K27me3). 12–14 cycles for lower-abundance targets (transcription factors, CTCF). Too many cycles introduces PCR duplicates that inflate apparent signal. When in doubt, use 12 and check duplicate rate after sequencing.









ImportantChecklist




Adapters used: _______
Qubit HS dsDNA: _______ ng/µL
Tapestation peak: _______ bp (expected 150–350 bp)
Submitted for sequencing: _______

title: CUT&RUN Protocol
text:

Checkpoint
Expected
Observed
Pass




Day 1: cells binding beads
Even suspension




Day 2: digest supernatant
Clear




Post-Zymo Qubit
>1 ng/µL




Post-ligation Qubit

Final library Qubit
>2 ng/µL




Tapestation peak

Notes / deviations:

Skene PJ, Henikoff JG, Henikoff S. eLife. 2018;7:e36906. Protocol: Phanstiel lab; adapted by JP Flores.

title: Lab notebook
text: This is a living record of wet lab experiments, protocols, and reading notes. It is not a computational notebook — that lives separately. Every experiment entry is one .qmd file per run, committed to git when complete.

title: Lab notebook
text:
Quick links



Section
What’s in it




Protocols
Shiny QMDs — configure inputs, print to PDF, take to bench


Experiments
One entry per experiment run, filed by date


Reading notes
Protocol-adjacent papers worth annotating in depth


Aims
F32, HFSP, Fulbright — living documents

title: Lab notebook
text:
How to use this notebook
Starting a new experiment:
# Copy the relevant template
cp experiments/_templates/HiC_entry_template.qmd \
   experiments/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)_HiC-[target]-[celltype].qmd

# Open in Positron and fill in the planning section
# Then each evening: fill in that day's section, save

# When the experiment is fully done:
git add experiments/YYYY-MM-DD_HiC-target-celltype.qmd
git commit -m "Hi-C [target] [celltype] — [one-line result summary]"
Printing a protocol:
quarto serve protocols/HiC_protocol.qmd
# Configure Shiny inputs in browser → Cmd+P → Save as PDF
Rebuilding the site:
quarto render
# Open _site/index.html in browser

title: Lab notebook
text:
Recent experiments
No experiment entries yet. Run your first experiment!

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text: 🖨 Print / Save as PDF

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Configure experiment


Code
viewof n_wells    = Inputs.number({label: "6-well plate wells", value: 6, min: 1, max: 96, step: 1})
viewof hpsc_line  = Inputs.select(["H9 (WA09)", "UCSF4", "WTC11", "Custom"], {label: "hPSC line"})
viewof pp121      = Inputs.toggle({label: "PP121 — NOS1⁺ enrichment (Days 15–20)", value: false})

























Code
CM2 = 9.5
total_cm2 = n_wells * CM2
md`**${n_wells} well(s) · ${total_cm2} cm² total · ${hpsc_line}**
All master mix volumes include +10% excess.`

























Code
pp121 ? md`::: {.callout-warning}
**PP121 active.** Add PP121 at 1–2 µM from Day 15 to Day 20 only. Enriches NOS1⁺ nitrergic neurons at the expense of CHAT⁺ cholinergic neurons. Remove completely from EN-C on Day 20 — extended treatment reduces viability.
:::` : md``

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Media compositions






NoteMath audit — how every volume is calculated





Surface area — the foundation of all ENS volume calculations:
\[A_{total} = N_{wells} \times 9.5 \; cm^2/well\]
Cocktail volumes:



Phase
Days
µL/cm²




Cocktails A & B
0–4
200


Cocktail C early
6–8
400


Cocktail C late
10
600


EN-C feeds
15–40
200


EN-C late feeds
40+
400



Factor dilutions — each growth factor is added as a small volume to E6 base. For Cocktail A, BMP4 at 1:10,000 dilution means: \(V_{BMP4} = V_{total} \times 10^{-4}\)
NC-C medium — scales by number of ULA wells (2 mL per well): \[V_{NC\text{-}C\;total} = N_{wells} \times 2 \; mL\]
Coating volumes (PLO, LM/FN) — 100 µL/cm²: \[V_{coating} = A_{total} \times 0.1 \; mL/cm^2\]





Code
fmt_ens = (x, d = 1) => x.toFixed(d)








Cocktail A — Days 0–2 (200 µL/cm²)






NoteWhy these signals?





BMP4 (1 ng/mL) activates the NC gene regulatory network in the absence of serum. SB431542 blocks TGFβ to prevent mesendodermal fate. CHIR at 600 nM activates WNT to initiate NC specification. Lower CHIR here than in later cocktails — high WNT too early overspecifies posterior identity before NC identity is established.





Code
{
  const vol = total_cm2 * 0.2
  Inputs.table([
    {Component: "Essential 6 (E6)",    Catalog: "Life Tech A1516401", "Final conc.": "base",    "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * (1 - 1e-4 - 1e-3 - 1e-4))},
    {Component: "BMP4 (10 µg/mL)",    Catalog: "R&D 314-BP",        "Final conc.": "1 ng/mL", "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 1e-4)},
    {Component: "SB431542 (10 mM)",   Catalog: "R&D 1614",          "Final conc.": "10 µM",   "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 1e-3)},
    {Component: "CHIR 99021 (6 mM)",  Catalog: "Tocris 4423",       "Final conc.": "600 nM",  "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 1e-4)}
  ], {layout: "auto"})
}









Cocktail B — Days 2–4 (200 µL/cm²)






NoteWhy the CHIR increase?





CHIR rises from 600 nM to 1.5 µM at Day 2. Once NC identity is initiated, sustained higher WNT signaling drives proliferation and maintains SOX10+ progenitor state. BMP4 is dropped — it is only needed in the short induction window at Day 0.





Code
{
  const vol = total_cm2 * 0.2
  Inputs.table([
    {Component: "Essential 6 (E6)",   "Final conc.": "base",   "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * (1 - 1e-3 - 2.5e-4))},
    {Component: "SB431542 (10 mM)",  "Final conc.": "10 µM",  "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 1e-3)},
    {Component: "CHIR 99021 (6 mM)", "Final conc.": "1.5 µM", "Amount (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 2.5e-4)}
  ], {layout: "auto"})
}









Cocktail C — Days 6–12 (vagal patterning + retinoic acid)






WarningVolume increases — do not miss this




Days 6–8: 400 µL/cm² (doubles from Cocktail B)
Day 10: 600 µL/cm² (increases again)

This compensates for rising confluency. Insufficient volume at this stage causes monolayer detachment and lost cells.








WarningRetinoic acid handling



RA is light-sensitive and degrades rapidly at 37°C. Always make a fresh working stock from your –80°C aliquot on the day of use. Use amber tubes. Do not re-use opened aliquots.








NoteWhy RA starts at Day 6, not Day 0





Premature RA exposure (Days 0–4) actually inhibits NC induction by over-posteriorizing cells before NC identity is established. RA is only added once CHIR-driven NC specification is underway, then it caudalizes NC cells toward vagal/HOXB3+/HOXB5+ identity required for ENS competence.





Code
{
  const v6  = total_cm2 * 0.4
  const v10 = total_cm2 * 0.6
  Inputs.table([
    {Component: "Essential 6 (E6)",       "Final conc.": "base",   "Days 6–8 (µL)": fmt_ens(v6  * (1 - 1e-3 - 2.5e-4 - 1e-3)), "Day 10 (µL)": fmt_ens(v10 * (1 - 1e-3 - 2.5e-4 - 1e-3))},
    {Component: "SB431542 (10 mM)",      "Final conc.": "10 µM",  "Days 6–8 (µL)": fmt_ens(v6  * 1e-3),                         "Day 10 (µL)": fmt_ens(v10 * 1e-3)},
    {Component: "CHIR 99021 (6 mM)",     "Final conc.": "1.5 µM", "Days 6–8 (µL)": fmt_ens(v6  * 2.5e-4),                       "Day 10 (µL)": fmt_ens(v10 * 2.5e-4)},
    {Component: "Retinoic Acid (1 mM)",  "Final conc.": "1 µM",   "Days 6–8 (µL)": fmt_ens(v6  * 1e-3),                         "Day 10 (µL)": fmt_ens(v10 * 1e-3)}
  ], {layout: "auto"})
}









NC-C — Crestosphere medium (Days 12–15, 2 mL/ULA well)






TipFGF2 stability



FGF2 is unstable at 37°C. Add it from a fresh –80°C aliquot to NC-C on the day of use. Carry protein in 0.1% BSA to prevent adsorption to tube walls.




Code
{
  const vol = n_wells * 2  // mL
  Inputs.table([
    {Component: "Neurobasal medium", Catalog: "Life Tech 21103049", "Per mL": "base",                "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 1e3 * (1 - 0.01 - 0.02 - 0.01 - 0.01 - 0.001 - 5e-4))},
    {Component: "N2 (100×)",        Catalog: "CTS A1370701",       "Per mL": "10 µL",              "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 10)},
    {Component: "B27 (50×)",        Catalog: "Life Tech 17504044", "Per mL": "20 µL",              "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 20)},
    {Component: "Glutagro (200 mM)","Catalog": "Corning 25-015-CI","Per mL": "10 µL",              "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 10)},
    {Component: "MEM NEAAs (100×)", Catalog: "Corning 25-025-CI", "Per mL": "10 µL",              "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 10)},
    {Component: "FGF2 (10 µg/mL)", Catalog: "R&D 233-FB",        "Per mL": "1 µL → 10 ng/mL",   "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 1)},
    {Component: "CHIR 99021 (6 mM)","Catalog": "Tocris 4423",     "Per mL": "0.5 µL → 3 µM",     "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 0.5)}
  ], {layout: "auto"})
}









EN-C — Enteric neuron maturation medium (Days 15+)






TipAfter Day 40



Reduce feeding to 1–2×/week but increase volume to 400 µL/cm². Wait until medium turns yellow before feeding — over-feeding mature cultures stresses neurons. If cultures begin detaching, supplement EN-C with FN (2 µg/mL) + LM (2 µg/mL).




Code
{
  const vol = total_cm2 * 0.2
  Inputs.table([
    {Component: "Neurobasal medium",     Catalog: "Life Tech 21103049", "Per mL": "base",               "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * (1 - 0.01 - 0.02 - 0.01 - 0.01 - 0.001 - 5e-4))},
    {Component: "N2 (100×)",            Catalog: "CTS A1370701",       "Per mL": "10 µL",             "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 0.01)},
    {Component: "B27 (50×)",            Catalog: "Life Tech 17504044", "Per mL": "20 µL",             "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 0.02)},
    {Component: "Glutagro (200 mM)",    Catalog: "Corning 25-015-CI", "Per mL": "10 µL",             "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 0.01)},
    {Component: "MEM NEAAs (100×)",     Catalog: "Corning 25-025-CI", "Per mL": "10 µL",             "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 0.01)},
    {Component: "GDNF (10 µg/mL)",     Catalog: "Peprotech 450-10",  "Per mL": "1 µL → 10 ng/mL",  "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 0.001)},
    {Component: "Ascorbic Acid (200 mM)","Catalog": "Sigma",          "Per mL": "0.5 µL → 100 µM",  "Per feed (µL)": fmt_ens(vol * 5e-4)}
  ], {layout: "auto"})
}

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Surface coating — PLO / Laminin / Fibronectin






WarningPrepare on Day 14 — not Day 15



The PLO overnight step means coating must start the day before plating. If you prepare it Day 15 morning you will lose hours of LM/FN incubation time and get poor cell attachment.








WarningNever let the coated surface dry



Aspirate the LM/FN solution and immediately add cells — no gap. A dried laminin surface is denatured and cells will not adhere.




Code
{
  const vol = total_cm2 * 0.1
  Inputs.table([
    {Step: "1 — PLO overnight",           Reagent: `PLO 15 µg/mL in borate buffer — ${fmt_ens(vol, 0)} µL`, Temp: "37°C",  Time: "O/N"},
    {Step: "2 — Wash",                    Reagent: "PBS × 3",                                                  Temp: "RT",    Time: "—"},
    {Step: "3 — LM + FN (≥2 hr)",        Reagent: `Laminin 2 µg/mL + FN 2 µg/mL in PBS — ${fmt_ens(vol, 0)} µL`, Temp: "37°C", Time: "≥2 hr"},
    {Step: "4 — Aspirate before plating", Reagent: "Do NOT let surface dry",                                   Temp: "—",     Time: "—"}
  ], {layout: "auto"})
}

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Phase 1 — ENCC induction (Days –2 to 12)

Day –2: Replate hPSCs






ImportantDay 0 confluency is the #1 determinant of induction efficiency



Target: ~70% confluent monolayer on Day 0. Too sparse (<60%) or too dense (>85%) both significantly reduce CD49D+ yield at Day 12. If your cell line tends to grow slowly, plate at 5:4 instead of 5:6.










Step
Action
Detail




1
Aspirate E8-C; EDTA (500 µM in PBS) 3–5 min
Longer than standard passaging — increases cell separation


2
Passage at 5:6 ratio (5 wells → 6 new wells)
—


3
Plate on Matrigel or Vitronectin XF in E8-C
Vitronectin preferred for fully defined system


4
Target: ~70% confluent monolayer on Day 0
Feed every other day with E8-C





Days 0–12: Medium change schedule






TipAdding medium without detaching cells



As the monolayer gets dense (especially Days 8–12), always tilt the plate and pipette slowly from the lower corner or side. Never pipette directly downward onto confluent cells — shear force detaches them.




Code
Inputs.table([
  {Day: "0",     Medium: "Cocktail A",  "µL/cm²": "200", "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.2, 0), Notes: "Switch from E8; record Day 0"},
  {Day: "2",     Medium: "Cocktail B",  "µL/cm²": "200", "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.2, 0), Notes: "—"},
  {Day: "4",     Medium: "Cocktail B",  "µL/cm²": "200", "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.2, 0), Notes: "Full medium change"},
  {Day: "6",     Medium: "Cocktail C",  "µL/cm²": "400", "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.4, 0), Notes: "⚠ Volume increases; add RA"},
  {Day: "8",     Medium: "Cocktail C",  "µL/cm²": "400", "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.4, 0), Notes: "Tilt plate; add from corner"},
  {Day: "10",    Medium: "Cocktail C",  "µL/cm²": "600", "Total (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.6, 0), Notes: "⚠ Volume increases again"},
  {Day: "11–12", Medium: "Harvest →",   "µL/cm²": "—",   "Total (µL)": "—",                          Notes: "QC first; proceed to Phase 2"}
], {layout: "auto"})









Day 11–12 QC — do not proceed without passing






ImportantQC gate — required before Phase 2




FACS: CD49D+ ≥ 50% (target 60–80%) — observed: _______ %
qPCR: HOXB3 ≥ 10-fold over Day 0 hPSC — observed: _______ fold
qPCR: HOXB5 ≥ 10-fold over Day 0 hPSC — observed: _______ fold
Phase contrast: loose, elongated cells; disrupted colony borders

If CD49D+ < 40%, check RA freshness, CHIR lot, and Day 0 confluency before troubleshooting further.








TipOptional: FACS purify at Day 12 for early progenitor experiments



Sort for CD49D+ using Pe/Cy7 anti-human CD49D (Biolegend 304314). Recommended if you need pure ENC progenitors for Day 11 assays. For most applications, the sphere phase (Days 12–15) provides sufficient enrichment without sorting.

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Phase 2 — Crestosphere formation (Days 12–15)






WarningUltra-low attachment plates — must be certified ULA



Standard tissue culture plates will cause cells to adhere instead of forming spheres. Use Corning Costar certified ultra-low attachment plates only. This is the most common cause of Phase 2 failure.




Code
Inputs.table([
  {Step: "1", Action: "Aspirate Cocktail C",                                                                       Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "2", Action: `Accutase: ${fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.1, 0)} µL (100 µL/cm²); 30 min 37°C`,                  Detail: "Full 30 min — do not shorten"},
  {Step: "3", Action: `Add NC-C: ${fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.1, 0)} µL; harvest with serological pipette`,           Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "4", Action: "Spin 290×g 1 min RT; resuspend in NC-C",                                                    Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "5", Action: `Transfer to ULA plates: 2 mL/well × ${n_wells} wells`,                                     Detail: "Certified ULA only — Corning Costar"},
  {Step: "6", Action: "Incubate 2 days",                                                                            Detail: "—"},
  {Step: "7", Action: "Day 14: swirl to center; aspirate from circumference carefully",                             Detail: "Never aspirate spheroids with media"},
  {Step: "8", Action: "Add 2 mL fresh NC-C/well; incubate 24h",                                                    Detail: "Target: 100–300 µm, compact, round"}
], {layout: "auto"})













TipSphere size troubleshooting



Spheres should be 100–300 µm at Day 14, compact and round with smooth borders. If spheres have fused into large irregular bodies, gently pipette 2–3× with a P1000 to separate. Necrotic cores develop in large bodies and reduce Day 15 viability.








ImportantDay 15 QC




FACS: CD49D+ ≥ 80–90% — observed: _______ %

If CD49D+ is still <70% after the sphere phase, extend suspension culture to Day 16 before plating.

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Phase 3 — 2D ENS differentiation (Days 15–40+)






WarningSerological pipette — not P1000 — for Day 15 dissociation



The narrower P1000 tip adds shear stress that causes excessive cell death. Use a 5 mL serological pipette for gentle dissociation into small clusters (5–20 cells). Single cell suspensions give very poor neuronal yields.




Code
{
  const baseSteps = [
    {Step: "1", Action: "Prepare PLO/LM/FN coating on Day 14 (see Surface Coating section)",                       Detail: "Do not let surface dry"},
    {Step: "2", Action: "Swirl ULA plates; remove NC-C from circumference with P1000",                              Detail: "—"},
    {Step: "3", Action: "Wash with PBS; add Accutase (1 mL/well); 30 min 37°C",                                    Detail: "—"},
    {Step: "4", Action: "⚠ USE 5 mL SEROLOGICAL PIPETTE — 2–3 gentle rounds",                                      Detail: "Small clusters only; P1000 causes excess cell death"},
    {Step: "5", Action: "Spin 290×g 1 min; count viable cells with hemocytometer + trypan blue",                   Detail: "—"},
    {Step: "6", Action: `Plate 100,000 viable cells/cm² — ${fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 1e5 / 1e6, 1)}M cells total`,    Detail: "Move plates N/S/E/W after returning to incubator"}
  ]
  const pp121Step = {Step: "7", Action: "⚠ Add PP121 (1–2 µM) — NOS1⁺ enrichment", Detail: "Days 15–20 ONLY. Remove from EN-C Day 20. Depletes CHAT⁺ cholinergic neurons."}
  Inputs.table(pp121 ? [...baseSteps, pp121Step] : baseSteps, {layout: "auto"})
}













NoteWhy move plates N/S/E/W after plating?





Cells in suspension in EN-C distribute unevenly as soon as the plate is set flat. A gentle 4-direction rocking motion redistributes them before they settle, giving even coverage across the well. Do this within 2 minutes of placing in the incubator.




Feeding schedule


Code
Inputs.table([
  {"Day range": "15–40", Frequency: "Every other day", "µL/cm²": "200", "Total/feed (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.2, 0), Notes: "Tilt; add from corner — never onto neurites"},
  {"Day range": "40–75", Frequency: "1–2×/week",       "µL/cm²": "400", "Total/feed (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.4, 0), Notes: "Wait for yellow before feeding"},
  {"Day range": ">75",   Frequency: "When yellow",     "µL/cm²": "400", "Total/feed (µL)": fmt_ens(total_cm2 * 0.4, 0), Notes: "Supplement with FN+LM if detaching"}
], {layout: "auto"})













TipLong-term culture (Day 75+)



For cultures older than 50 days, wait until medium turns yellow before feeding — do not feed on a fixed schedule. Use FN/LM-supplemented EN-C at every feed. Cultures lasting >100 days are achievable. The main enemy of long-term cultures is aggressive medium changes that mechanically detach neurites.

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
QC checkpoints summary



Day
Checkpoint
Expected
Observed
Pass




0
hPSC confluency
~70% monolayer




6
SOX10 clustering
GFP+ clusters visible




12
ENCC induction (FACS)
CD49D+ ≥50%




12
Vagal identity (qPCR)
HOXB3/5 ≥10-fold




15
Sphere enrichment (FACS)
CD49D+ ≥80–90%




15
Sphere morphology
100–300 µm, compact, round




30
Early neurons (IF)
TUBB3+/TrkC+ networks




40
Neuronal purity (FACS)
>80% TUBB3+




40
Neurochemical (IF)
NOS1, CHAT, 5HT, GABA




75
Full FACS
CHAT~40%, NOS1~9%, 5HT~6%











NoteExpected neurochemical composition at Day 75





Per Barber et al. 2019 Fig. 4g and Fig. 5b: ~40% CHAT+ (cholinergic), ~9% GABA+, ~9% NOS1+ (nitrergic), ~6% 5HT+ (serotonergic), and ~25% SOX10+/GFAP+ (enteric glia). With PP121 treatment Days 15–20, NOS1+ increases 3–5× at the expense of CHAT+ (Majd et al. 2025).



Notes / deviations:

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
Troubleshooting






TipCD49D+ <40% at Day 12





Most likely causes: wrong Day 0 confluency; degraded RA; CHIR lot variability. Check in this order: (1) confirm Day 0 was 70%; (2) make fresh RA from a new –80°C aliquot; (3) titrate your CHIR lot (1–5 µM) in a test well. CHIR activity varies significantly between lots.









TipSpheroids not forming (Phase 2)





Almost always a plate issue — verify Corning ULA certification. If using correct plates, check FGF2 activity (add BSA carrier) and reduce seeding density.









TipLow TUBB3+ at Day 40 (<60%)





Three most common causes: (1) single-cell dissociation at Day 15 — use serological pipette; (2) PLO/LM/FN surface dried before plating; (3) inactive GDNF lot. Test each in order.









TipSMA+ myofibroblast contamination





NC-derived SMA+ flat cells are the primary contaminant. They eventually cause neuronal detachment. Minimize by FACS-sorting CD49D+ at Day 12 if contamination is severe, or by reducing passaging ratio at Day –2.

title: 2D ENS Differentiation Protocol
text:
References
Barber K, Studer L, Fattahi F. Nat Protoc. 2019;14(4):1261–1279.
Majd H, Samuel RM, et al. Nature. 2025;645:158–167.
Fattahi F, et al. Nature. 2016;531:105–109.

title: Protocols
text: Interactive protocol calculators — set your sample count and volumes scale automatically. Use 🖨 Print / Save as PDF on any protocol for a bench copy.

title: Protocols
text:
Available protocols



Protocol
Type
Source




In situ Hi-C

title: Protocols
text:
Adding a new protocol
Give Claude the source (PDF, .docx, or paste the methods section) and say “make a QMD for this protocol.” Output goes in protocols/. Then add a row to the table above.
